displayTitle: The System That Lies When You Stop Checking

beats.narration: Magnetic particle testing fails quietly. The lamp glows, the bath flows, sensitivity drifts away — and nothing warns you. The daily QC routine is your defense.

payload.title: CATCH THE DRIFT
payload.sub: An MT system degrades invisibly — QC is the only thing that catches it.

beats.narration: Concentration starts here: draw a hundred-milliliter sample, let it settle, and read the stem — fluorescent baths want point-one to point-four milliliters.

payload.heading: The settling test
payload.note: 100 mL FROM THE HOSE · READ THE GRADUATED STEM

beats.narration: The hardware drifts too. Verify the ammeter within ten percent, the yoke's lift — ten pounds AC, forty DC — and a thousand microwatts UV.

payload.heading: Check the hardware, by the numbers

beats.narration: Then prove the field: a pie gauge shows direction, a gaussmeter reads thirty to sixty gauss, and a pocket indicator confirms residual below three.

payload.heading: Prove the field, then the part

beats.narration: Run the routine every shift and write down every reading. The system never gets a chance to lie to you.

payload.sub: The system never gets a chance to lie to you.
